I read the prompt and prior investigations as external proposals. The surviving candidate is still Turn 9 / Turn 10‚Äôs relation, not the earlier candidates refuted in Turns 3, 6, and 8. The prompt‚Äôs required syntax is the four-generator tame format with implicit ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and constants reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior file correctly records the chain of failures and repairs leading to the current candidate.

The candidate remains:sigma,tau,x,y nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

The constants check out:ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąí2ŌÄ ‚Č°50932630783(modM).The Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg proof pattern really does require verifying all finite tame subextensions as DemuŇ°kin groups with the correct invariants, not just matching H1 -rank; Neukirch‚Äôs report describes exactly this DemuŇ°kin-formation strategy for the known pÓÄ†=2 case, and Jarden‚ÄďShusterman record both the pÓÄ†=2 explicit-description result and the fact that Diekert‚Äôs dyadic result does not cover K=Q2.Numdam+1 Schneider also explicitly identifies the remaining ramified dyadic case as the difficult unknown case and uses norm terms of the form (xŌĄ)ŌÄ in her dyadic computations.University of Regensburg A correction to Turn 10: its trivial-ŌĄ block proof has the right conclusion but the displayed division is reversed in the even-f case. Iff=2a m,m¬†odd,s=ŌÄmodf,then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). In the trivial tame-inertia block the linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

For even f, since s is odd, 1+T divides 1+T‚ąís, so the correct substitution is

Y=P(T)X+YňČ,P(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,not (1+T)/(1+T‚ąís). The same unit argument still works. The reduced X-block multiplier is h(T)=1+P(T)+P(T‚ąí1).

For a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=(1+ő∂)+(1+ő∂‚ąís)+ő∂(1+ő∂s)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1.

Thus h(ő∂)=0 would imply ő∂2s+1=1. But 2s+1‚Č°1(modm)and 2s+1 is odd, so gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence no nontrivial f-th root is a zero of h, and h(1)=1. So Turn 10‚Äôs nondegeneracy result survives, but the corrected quotient is important for the next layer.The new calculation this turn is the mod-4 orientation/Bockstein class. For the finite tame quotientőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,write Xg,Yg for the Schreier classes of x,y, and S=ŌÉf. Defineőļe,f=g‚ąąőĒe,f‚ąĎXg‚ą®+g‚ąąőĒe,f‚ąĎYg‚ą®,őļe,f (S)=0.

Equivalently,őļe,f(Xg)=1,őļe,f(Yg)=1,őļe,f (S)=0 for every g‚ąąőĒe,f. This character satisfies the linearized relators because the x-coefficient isŌÉňČ‚ąís+őĽ, the y-coefficient is 1+őĽŌÉňČ, and both evaluate to 1+1=0 on the constant vector, where őĽ=e‚ąí1‚ąĎi=0e‚ąí1ŌĄňČi.

The Magnus-degree calculation gives ő≤(Ōē)=Ōē‚ą™Ōē=Ōē‚ą™őļe,f for¬†all¬†Ōē‚ąąH1(Ue,f,F2).

So őļe,f is the candidate‚Äôs Kummer class of ‚ąí1, i.e. the mod-4 cyclotomic-orientation character. This matches the base computation: forr=xz‚ąí1 xz[z,y],the index-two subgroup with C4 -torsion is not ker(x‚ą®), but ker(x‚ą®+y‚ą®),exactly the f=e=1 specialization of őļe,f. This also explains why the Turn 1/2 relation could pass the cup-rank test but fail the cyclotomic test: it had the wrong mod-4 orientation class.I also pushed the next Smith-form check on the unique orientation kernel. LetVe,f=kerőļe,f.After eliminating the nontrivial tame-inertia blocks and the augmentation blocks whose matrices are 2-adic units, the only torsion-producing block is the invariant three-generator block. Up to multiplication by odd 2-adic units it is the same block as the base relationR=XZ‚ąí1 XZ[Z,Y].

For the kernel of X‚ą®+Y‚ą®, using transversal {1,X}, the abelianized Reidemeister‚ÄďSchreier matrix reduces to(‚ąí22112‚ąí2),with extra zero columns for free generators. Its Smith form over Z2is diag(1,4).

Thus the orientation kernel has torsion C4, not C8, in the invariant block. This is precisely the obstruction that killed the Turn 1/2 candidate in Turn 3, and this candidate avoids it.What I would not yet call fully proved is the global profinite identification theorem. The current candidate now has, for every finite tame (e,f), the correct H1 -rank, nondegenerate DemuŇ°kin cup product, corrected trivial and nontrivial tame-inertia cup blocks, the explicit mod-4 orientation classőļe,f=NőĒ(X‚ą®+Y‚ą®),and the C4, not C8, Smith block for the unique orientation kernel. That is very strong evidence and closes the main remaining arithmetic invariant from Turn 10. The remaining task is packaging this into a dyadic replacement for the Jannsen‚ÄďWingberg formation uniqueness step, since the standard pÓÄ†=2 formation argument is exactly what is unavailable in the ramified dyadic case.

ESTABLISHED: Corrected Turn 10‚Äôs trivial-block algebra: P=(1+T‚ąís)/(1+T), with the same unit proof for h=1+P+P‚ąó. Computed the candidate‚Äôs uniform mod-4 orientation class:őļe,f=g‚ąĎXg‚ą®+g‚ąĎYg‚ą®.Showed the unique orientation kernel reduces to the base invariant Smith block diag(1,4), so the candidate avoids the spurious C8 obstruction.

GAP: The candidate is now DemuŇ°kin- and orientation-correct at the finite tame pro-2 level, but a complete solution still needs a formal global uniqueness/formation argument in the ramified dyadic setting, or an equivalent proof that these finite tame checks force the full absolute Galois group.

NEXT: Write the finite-tame verification as a theorem: define Ue,f, prove it is a DemuŇ°kin group with orientation character őļe,f and q(Ve,f)=4, then formulate the missing dyadic ‚Äúformation‚ÄĚ uniqueness statement needed to turn the candidate into a final presentation.[BREAKTHROUGH]
